```{r}
library(pacman)
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, ggplot2, emmeans)
options(scipen = 999)
```

```{r}
# load the data
romance_bias_data <- read_csv("data/associational_gender_bias/romance_associational_gender_bias.csv")

sci_fi_bias_data <- read_csv("data/associational_gender_bias/sci_fi_associational_gender_bias.csv")

literary_fiction_bias_data <- read_csv("data/associational_gender_bias/literary_fiction_associational_gender_bias.csv")

# add genre column
romance_bias_data <- romance_bias_data %>%
  mutate(genre = "romance")

sci_fi_bias_data <- sci_fi_bias_data %>%
  mutate(genre = "sci-fi")

literary_fiction_bias_data <- literary_fiction_bias_data %>%
  mutate(genre = "literary fiction")

# bind data together
bias_data <- bind_rows(romance_bias_data, sci_fi_bias_data, literary_fiction_bias_data)
```

```{r}
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)

# Fit model as before
model <- lm(gender_bias ~ 0 + dimension * genre, data = bias_data)

# Pass robust standard errors to emmeans
emm <- emmeans(model, ~ dimension * genre, 
               vcov. = vcovHC(model, type = "HC3"))
test(emm)
```

```{r}
plot(residuals(model), type = "l")  # look for trends or patterns over index
```

```{r}
library(lmtest)
bptest(model)  # Breusch-Pagan test, H0 = homoscedasticity
```

```{r}
# QQ plot
plot(model, which = 2)

# Histogram of residuals
hist(residuals(model), breaks = 30, main = "Histogram of Residuals", 
     xlab = "Residuals")

# Formal test
shapiro.test(residuals(model))  # H0 = normality
                                # note: very sensitive with large samples
```
